tools.name: deep_research
tools.description: Conduct multi-angle deep research on a topic by running several focused web searches and compiling a comprehensive source collection. Returns a structured set of sources, summaries, and key findings organized by research angle. Best used when the user asks for a thorough investigation, background research, or wants to understand a topic in depth — not for simple one-shot lookups.

question.description: The research question or topic to investigate thoroughly

sourcesPerAngle.description: Number of web results to fetch per search angle (1–8, default: 5)

angles.description: Optional: explicit search queries to use as research angles. If omitted, angles are derived automatically from the question (up to 3).

useWhen: the user wants a thorough, multi-source investigation of a topic
the user asks to 'research', 'deep dive', or 'dig into' something in depth
a single web search would not give sufficient breadth or coverage
avoidWhen: the user only needs a quick one-shot lookup — use web_search instead
the user is asking about a simple factual question
typicalInputs: research the current state of AI reasoning models
deep dive into Azure Durable Functions best practices
investigate the competitive landscape for TypeScript ORMs
research what's happening with SpaceX Starship development
tools.returnsSummaryShape: structured multi-angle research with sources, titles, snippets, and findings organized by research angle

shortDescription: Multi-angle web research — fans out across several search queries to gather comprehensive source material on a topic

externalAccountsNeeded.howToObtain: Free tier available at https://api.search.brave.com/; key must be stored in stamp Key Vault as BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY

orchestratorUseCases: conduct thorough multi-source research on a topic the user wants to understand in depth
